sql: SELECT * FROM trips WHERE started_at > '2024-01-01' LIMIT 100
SELECT start_station_name, COUNT(*) AS rides FROM trips GROUP BY start_station_name ORDER BY rides DESC LIMIT 10
SELECT APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT(ride_id) AS approx_rides FROM trips
SELECT start_station_name, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN member_casual = 'casual' THEN ride_id END) AS casual_rides, COUNT(*) AS total FROM trips GROUP BY start_station_name
SELECT DATE_TRUNC(started_at, MONTH) AS month, COUNT(*) AS rides FROM trips GROUP BY month ORDER BY month
SELECT ride_id FROM trips WHERE started_at > TIMESTAMP_SUB(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), INTERVAL 7 DAY)
SELECT ride_id, start_station_name, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY start_station_name ORDER BY started_at) AS rn FROM trips
SELECT ride_id, start_station_name FROM trips QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY start_station_name ORDER BY started_at DESC) = 1
SELECT ride_id, DATE_DIFF(ended_at, started_at, MINUTE) AS duration_min FROM trips

WITH monthly AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC(started_at, MONTH) AS month, member_casual, COUNT(DISTINCT ride_id) AS rides FROM trips GROUP BY month, member_casual) SELECT month, SUM(CASE WHEN member_casual = 'member' THEN rides END) AS members, SUM(CASE WHEN member_casual = 'casual' THEN rides END) AS casuals FROM monthly GROUP BY month
SELECT ride_id FROM trips EXCEPT DISTINCT SELECT ride_id FROM trips_archive
WITH RECURSIVE counter AS (SELECT 1 AS n UNION ALL SELECT n + 1 FROM counter WHERE n < 10) SELECT n FROM counter
SELECT * FROM (SELECT ride_id, started_at FROM trips ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 500) AS recent WHERE started_at > '2024-06-01'
SELECT ride_id, CASE WHEN duration_min < 5 THEN 'short' WHEN duration_min < 30 THEN 'medium' WHEN duration_min < 120 THEN 'long' ELSE 'extended' END AS bucket FROM trips
SELECT t.ride_id, s.station_name FROM trips AS t JOIN stations AS s ON t.start_station_id = s.station_id WHERE t.started_at > '2024-01-01'

WITH base AS (SELECT ride_id, start_station_name, started_at FROM trips WHERE started_at > '2024-01-01'), agg AS (SELECT start_station_name, COUNT(*) AS rides FROM base GROUP BY start_station_name) SELECT * FROM agg ORDER BY rides DESC LIMIT 10
SELECT start_station_name, member_casual, COUNT(*) AS rides FROM trips GROUP BY ROLLUP(start_station_name, member_casual)
SELECT start_station_name, COUNT(*) AS rides FROM trips GROUP BY start_station_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 100
SELECT ride_id, ended_at FROM trips ORDER BY ended_at DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 50
SELECT ride_id FROM trips WHERE start_station_id IS DISTINCT FROM end_station_id
